name: Potsdamer Platz U-Bahn Underpass
canCrossText: CAN cross via underground U-Bahn passages throughout race. Follow signs to different exits to emerge on opposite side of course.
sideOutcomeText: West exit → Potsdamer Straße side (west of course). East exit → Leipziger Straße side (east of course).
trapRiskText: HIGH trap risk at surface level. Course barriers and construction make surface crossing impossible. Spectators who exit at the wrong surface exit get stranded.
notes: This is the single most important crossing hack for Berlin Marathon spectators. The Straßenunterführungen der U-Bahnhöfe allow passage beneath the race course.

name: Yorckstraße Bahnunterführung
locationText: Yorckstraße, beneath railway bridges near Yorckstraße S-Bahn
canCrossText: CAN cross via vehicle underpass beneath the course. Pedestrian access available alongside vehicular tunnel. Open throughout race.
sideOutcomeText: North → Kreuzberg side. South → Schöneberg side. SIDE NOT FOUND for exact course-relative positioning — verify on race day.
trapRiskText: Low trap risk. Underpass is physically separated from course level.
notes: Bahnunterführung = railway underpass. Multiple parallel underpasses exist at Yorckstraße. The pedestrian path may be crowded.

name: Alexanderplatz Underground Passages
nearestStations: Alexanderplatz (U2, U5, U8, S5, S7, S75)
canCrossText: CAN cross via underground passages. Multiple exits available. However, specific exit numbering for course-side positioning is poorly documented.
sideOutcomeText: SIDE NOT FOUND — exit numbering is complex. Rathausstraße exit reported as better for southbound course access.
trapRiskText: MEDIUM trap risk. Large station with many exits; emerging at wrong exit wastes 10–15 min walking around barriers to correct position.
notes: Alexanderplatz is a massive interchange. The underground level provides course crossing but exit selection is critical.

name: Friedrichstraße Station Passages
locationText: Friedrichstraße, between Unter den Linden and Georgenstraße
nearestStations: Friedrichstraße (S1, S2, S5, S7, S25, U6)
canCrossText: CAN cross via station underground passages. North-south passage beneath tracks allows movement between Schiffbauerdamm and Georgenstraße sides.
sideOutcomeText: North exit → Spree river side. South exit → Unter den Linden side.
trapRiskText: Low trap risk if using underground. Surface-level crossing blocked by course barriers.
notes: Friedrichstraße station has a north-south pedestrian passage that effectively crosses the course corridor.

name: Tiergarten Tunnel (Straße des 17. Juni)
locationText: Beneath Straße des 17. Juni, near Großer Stern
canCrossText: CAN'T reliably cross during race. The tunnel entrances may be within the restricted finish corridor zone. STATUS UNCERTAIN — verify race-day signage.
trapRiskText: HIGH — approaching the finish corridor from any direction after 10:00 puts you in heavily restricted zones.
notes: The finish area around Straße des 17. Juni has the strictest access controls. Do not plan to use this as a crossing.

name: Bundesstraße / Hansaplatz Area
locationText: Near Hansaplatz U-Bahn, north side of Tiergarten
canCrossText: Designated spectator crossing points may be set up in this area, but timing windows are narrow and unpredictable. Typically open only between runner waves or when marshals allow brief gaps.
sideOutcomeText: SIDE NOT FOUND — depends on specific crossing point location
trapRiskText: MEDIUM — waiting for a gap can take 15–30 min during peak runner flow.
notes: Official designated crossings exist but their exact locations and timing vary by year. TIME NOT FOUND for specific opening windows.

name: Schöneberg U-Bahn Passages (U4/U7)
canCrossText: CAN cross via U-Bahn underground passages in Schöneberg. The course passes through this neighborhood and U-Bahn underpasses provide reliable crossing.
sideOutcomeText: Varies by exit. Check station maps for specific exit-to-course-side mapping.
trapRiskText: Low trap risk — this area is less congested than central zones.
notes: Southern portions of the course through Schöneberg are less documented for crossing but U-Bahn underpasses remain the reliable fallback.
